turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: Hi, doctor. I’m here for my blood pressure check, but I’ve also been getting headaches on and off.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Hello. Thanks for coming in. We’ll check both. How long have the headaches been going on, and where do you feel them?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: About two months now. Mostly at the front of my head, like a dull pressure. It comes and goes.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Do they come with nausea, blurred vision, weakness, or any numbness?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: No, none of that. Just a bit annoying. Sometimes I feel it after a long day at work.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Okay. Any fever, neck stiffness, head injury, or headaches that wake you from sleep?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: No fever or injury. They don’t wake me up. I usually notice them in the afternoon.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Tell me about your blood pressure medicine. Are you taking the amlodipine every day?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: Yes, I take it daily. I only miss it maybe once in a while if I’m rushing.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Any side effects from it, like ankle swelling, flushing, or dizziness?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: No, nothing really. Maybe a little tired sometimes, but I’m not sure if it’s the tablets.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: How has your lifestyle been lately? Things like salt intake, exercise, sleep, and stress can affect both blood pressure and headaches.

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: I’m mostly sitting at work all week. I know I don’t exercise enough. I do sleep okay, but work has been busy. I probably eat too much takeaway too.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: That helps. Do you smoke, or drink much alcohol?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: No smoking. I only drink socially on weekends, not a lot.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Thank you. I’ll check your blood pressure now. We’ll also look at your weight and talk about a plan that fits your routine.

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: Okay, that’s fine. I’ve been a bit worried because my mother also has high blood pressure.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: That family history is useful. Based on what you’ve told me, the headaches sound more like tension or lifestyle-related headaches than anything dangerous, but I’d like to confirm your blood pressure reading first. If it’s still high, we may need to adjust treatment. For now, keep taking amlodipine, try to reduce salt, drink enough water, and add regular walking during the week. If the headaches become severe, sudden, or come with vision changes, weakness, chest pain, or shortness of breath, seek urgent care. Otherwise, we’ll review the reading today and arrange follow-up soon.
